notes: Runs exactly the same as on Windows, which is not to say that it is perfect out of the box, but after installing SilentPatch there are no issues whatsoever. Any issues encountered while playing on Proton without SilentPatch applied will also be encountered on Windows.

title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

notes:
The game runs well enough, the performance is good and the loading is very fast, but after some time it will randomly crash when you enter/leave internal areas of the game, then you need to disable both Esync and DX_11, so in startup options you'll type: `PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %COMMAND%` and `PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %COMMAND%`. after that the game will runs with no crashs and freezing screen bugs, and i even run the game at 60fps with no issues.
 
side-note: I updated my report after seeing the fixes to the crashes,  and it's been working flawlessly.

title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

notes:
OK. This is a funny one. I disabled Esync, disabled DX10 and DX11. Also enabled large address aware.
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%
PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %COMMAND%
PROTON_NO_D3D10=1 %COMMAND%
With all this it seems to crash rarely at complete random and always when loading into a new area. For example between cutscenes or between entering or exiting buildings. The frame rate is a constant 25 - 27FPS, nothing I've tried changes it for better or worse.
I would give this a gold rating but, the odd rare crashes means every time you leave or enter a building, you worry the game may crash that 1 time in 100. Along with a sub 30FPS frame rate, that for me prevents me playing the game comfortably. The fact is, it still plays better than on modern Windows!
title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

notes: On Proton it works out of the box with WineD3D. If you use PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command% as a launch option (available since Proton 4.11) the game works great with D9VK (D3D9 to Vulkan translation). No graphical glitches anymore with D9VK since Proton 4.11-3. Tested with RADV and the new Mesa ACO compiler on Plasma Wayland.
title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

notes: Freezes sometimes when a cutscene starts. Game is playable, but this prevents it from being enjoyable.
title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

notes:
If you turn off frame limiter the game will freeze when loading cutscenes and transitions, but the game will lock at 25 fps. The only way to get higher FPS is to install Silent Patch mod which locks the game at a constant 30 fps, allows 16:9 aspect ratio, and fixes bugs. However, some features of Silent Patch will only be partially supported in the latest steam version, so downgrading the game is recommended. 
In order to downgrade GTA:SA, you would have to use this application from Rockstar Nexus, but you wont be able to connect to the Rockstar Nexus Servers to download the necessary files.  The only solution for this is to boot up windows and downgrade it there, then boot up Linux so you can transfer the files from Windows to Linux. You could try to use a VM to downgrade, but I didn't try this.

I tried to install every mod, but the game crashed when I started a new game. It worked when I just installed RemasteredGUI, SilentPatch, and Ginput. Another user also reported that 2dfx LOD mod doesn't work, so I would avoid downloading that. Some experimentation is required.

Heres the link to the downgrader, https://gtaforums.com/topic/927016-san-andreas-downgrader/
title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

notes: Works out of the box, but it was pretty crashy. Works perfectly after putting this as start options: "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_NO_D3D10=1 %command% -dx9"
title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

notes:
Plays well for the most part great. Sometimes I have issue with mouse input, but cycling through using different mouse input options (raw, etc.) generally fixes it in the moment.

Had the same major issue everybody else was facing with Rockstar's new launcher giving me "Unable to locate your Rockstar launcher, please validate your game data". Eventually reinstalled my distro for other reasons and came back to this game and then the launcher got stuck at the flashing Rockstar logo after throwing a couple errors. Used the same "No_GTAVLauncher.exe" fix others used at https://www.gta5-mods.com/tools/no-gtavlauncher. If you're having trouble finding PlayGTAV.exe, open up a terminal, type:

cd ~
find -name PlayGTAV.exe

And navigate there and replace PlayGTAV.exe with the no-launcher downloaded executable.
title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

notes:
As kindly suggested by other users before, just set PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command% as a launch option (available since Proton 4.11). 

Previously (using the other recommended settings like "disable esync" etc...) I had lots of random freezes (each under the 5 seconds threshold) especially in Los Santos when aiming or moving rapidly (the frame limiter was disabled, if enabled the game was unplayable). No irreversible crash ever occurred neither graphical glitches, however. 

Still, I had to downgrade the resolution down to 720p, with quality settings set to "high" and frame limiter disabled: FPS were not ideal yet.

Now, using only the D9VK option, the game runs smoothly @ 1080p, with "high" quality settings and frame limiter disabled! (you can even enable AA 2x, however expect some stutter in heavy populated zones in Los Santos (near Emmet for example)).

I run these new settings for more than an hour and the game is just perfect, thanks everyone!

The gaming experience changed completely, never thought I could game smoothly GTA SA on Linux and integrated graphics. Yay!
title: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas

notes: I ran the game using the "Enhanced Edition" compilation of mods, in order to get some graphic bug fixes, better textures, light effects, resolution fix, and so on... Here's the download link, and instructions for installing "Enhanced Edition": https://youtu.be/38OkRFX41K4 Obs.: After installing the mods, delete the "limit adjuster.ini and .asi" from the "scripts" folder. Otherwise the game won't pass the loading screen. After that, game runs 60FPS, max graphics, just like on Windows.
